title: UDS Component Content Schema
description: Schema for per-component spec JSON files. Every field is optional — the renderer hides sections where data is missing or empty. See uds-docs/content/<component>.json for examples.

description.description: One-paragraph summary of the component's purpose

acceptanceCriteria.description: Checklist items. This IS the ship checklist — when all items are verified (design + dev), the component can move to Production Ready.

tokens.description: Groups of CSS custom properties the component uses, e.g. { Surface: [...], Typography: [...] }

props.description: Web component attributes/props the Storybook implementation should support.

events.description: Custom events the component should dispatch.

slots.description: Named slots or content regions inside the component.

states.description: Every interactive state the component supports. Include 'not applicable' entries to make coverage explicit.

name.description: default | hover | active | focus | focus-visible | selected | disabled | error | loading | empty | not-applicable

hasVisual.description: Whether this state has a distinct visual treatment

contentGuidelines.description: Character limits, truncation, empty state copy, error tone

performance.description: Component-specific performance notes. Leave null unless there are specific concerns (e.g. virtualize Data Table at 200+ rows).

motion.description: DEFERRED: UDS has no motion tokens yet. Leave null.

responsive.description: DEFERRED: UDS has no breakpoint tokens yet. Leave null.

visualHierarchy.description: Where this component fits in page layout (primary action vs secondary, etc.)

densityBehavior.description: Appearance differences between compact vs comfortable density

draft.description: When true, hide from sidebar/routing on production. Show with ?draft=1.

figmaNodeId.description: Deep-link to specific variant node in Figma. If null, falls back to component page link.

storybookSlug.description: Storybook story path, e.g. 'components-button--primary'. If null, falls back to the Storybook home.
